reasoning:
The resolution fundamentally depends on comparing specific 30-day windows to a 360-day baseline (Nov 2024 - Nov 2025). 
1. The 30 days prior to 2025-11-16 (Oct 17 - Nov 16) perfectly captures the intense surge in violence following the October 27, 2025 announcement of President Paul Biya's re-election, which resulted in immediate lethal crackdowns, widespread arrests, and state incursions into neighborhoods during opposition 'lockdowns' (actions strongly pushing up the 'Violence against civilians' ACLED count). Because this peak is strictly contained within the 30-day window, its total will vastly exceed the 360-day baseline (which dilutes this single massive spike over a 12-month average).
2. The 30 days prior to 2025-12-09 (Nov 9 - Dec 9) will capture the tail-end of the election unrest, but primarily tracks mean-reverting normal background violence. Importantly, the target must beat a baseline that has been *structurally inflated* by the late-October/early-November violence. Probability remains above 50% only due to lingering unrest and high baseline right-skewness.
3. The 30 days prior to 2026-02-07 (Jan 8 - Feb 7) completely misses the election unrest and fortuitously ends just before the major seasonal violence associated with Youth Day (Feb 11). Thus, this window represents a standard 'normal' period. When a normal period is tested against a baseline inflated by a recent massive spike (the Oct 2025 election), mathematical expectation dictates the normal period will likely fall below the 360-day average. Thus probability falls below 50%.
Uncertainty values strictly align with the prompt requirements, narrowing towards base 0.5 as forecast horizons grow more distant.
